<response>
{
  "probabilities": {
    "team_a_win": 0.25,
    "draw": 0.15,
    "team_b_win": 0.60

}
</response>
<reasoning>
We need to produce probabilities for team A win, draw, team B win, predicted score, predicted winner, knockout_resolution fields (ended_in_extra_time false, ended_in_penalties false, penalty_shootout_score null) because group stage. Also confidence.

We must analyze form, rankings, stats.

Paraguay rank 41, avg goals 1.27 scored, 1.07 conceded. Recent form: mixed, 7 wins 3 draws 5 losses in last 15. Recent matches include a 0-1 loss to Turkey (but status says Win? Actually score 0-1, status Win? That seems contradictory; maybe they consider Paraguay win 0-1? Actually score "0-1" likely Paraguay 0, Turkey 1, so Paraguay lost. But status says Win. Data may be inconsistent. We'll treat overall stats.

Australia rank 27, better. Avg goals 1.33 scored, 0.87 conceded. Recent form: 9 wins 1 draw 5 losses (but 15 matches). Stronger defense.

Head-to-head not given.

We need to compute probabilities. Use ranking difference: Australia higher (27 vs 41). Use goal difference: Australia better.

But Paraguay has home advantage? Match in US neutral. Both are away.

We can assign base win probabilities: maybe Australia 55%, Paraguay 30%, draw 15%.

We need to calibrate with recent form: Paraguay recent 5 matches: loss to Turkey, loss to US 4-1, win vs Nicaragua 4-0, loss to Morocco 2-1, win vs Greece 0-1 (they won). So 2 wins, 3 losses. Not great.

Australia recent 5 matches: loss to US 2-0, win vs Turkey 2-0, draw vs Switzerland 1-1, loss to Mexico 1-0, win vs Curaçao 5-1. So 2 wins, 2 losses, 1 draw. Slightly better.

Thus probability maybe Australia 60%, Paraguay 25%, draw 15%.

Predicted score: maybe 2-0 for Australia.

Predicted winner: Australia.

Knockout resolution fields: all false, penalty_shootout_score null.

Confidence: maybe 0.68.

We must output JSON with floats.

Make sure sum of probabilities = 1.0 (within rounding). Use 0.60, 0.15, 0.25 sum 1.0.

Return JSON.


</reasoning>
